name: Artifact Model
summary: What artifacts are and how they flow through the studio
concise_summary: Artifacts are versioned work products. Always pass artifact IDs.

definitions.meaning: A versioned JSON object representing work product. Each artifact has an ID, type, lifecycle state, and content fields.
distinguishing_features: Created by agents, stored in stores (workspace, canon)
Has lifecycle: draft → review → approved → cold
Validated against its artifact type schema

definitions.meaning: A schema defining what fields an artifact contains and how it behaves.
Unique identifier for an artifact instance. Format: {type}_{random_suffix}
distinguishing_features: Used to fetch, reference, and pass artifacts between agents
Must be included in return_to_orchestrator calls

definitions.meaning: Lifecycle state indicating an artifact has passed quality gates and is ready for canon commitment.
distinguishing_features: Can still be edited if issues found before cold

rules.statement: Always include artifact IDs when returning to orchestrator
rules.reasoning: The orchestrator needs IDs to pass your work to the next agent. Without IDs, workflow breaks.

rules.statement: Use search_workspace to find artifacts, get_artifact to fetch by ID
rules.reasoning: Search for discovery, fetch for retrieval. Don't guess IDs.

procedures.goal: Pass work between agents via delegation
steps: Note artifact IDs from creation responses
Orchestrator passes IDs to next delegated agent

warnings.failure_mode: Referring to artifacts by description instead of ID
warnings.consequence: Next agent cannot find your work
warnings.prevention: Always capture and pass artifact IDs
